text:
There was once a farmer who had a pet goose that loved nothing more than feeding wild berries. One day the farmer discovered beautiful blueberries growing on an uncommon tree. He decided to grow them himself. The goose would spend hours feeding the time with the garden. One day, a raven flew down and ate every berry. The goose mourned greatly.

That evening, the owl said, "You poor thing. You're too smart for those blueberries. Come visit me over here." The goose happily took seeds to another garden. Sometimes regrets change things, but sometimes not.

That year, the berries bore berries. A whole flock of geese came each afternoon, feeding the goose. They all became wise from the pet goose.

One autumn, a wise old crow named Oliver visited. "I've seen those berries for many years," Oliver said. "They never change." The goose looked at him skeptically but then remembered those visits.

Suddenly, every autumn bird group on the moon came back
